mentions.note: Speculative stock; can allocate up to 10% of portfolio to speculation but not now while speculative wave unwinding; wait for margin panic to pass.
Down 50% YTD with 4.7% dividend yield and $7.5B buyback, but AI/cloud work duplicates Accenture services at much lower cost; fundamental threat despite valuation appeal — stay away.
China GDP disappointing (4.4%) but remain patient; down on dip; still best way to play China long-term; hold existing position.
Great company but perilous stock in margin panic; hedge funds shorting to pressure competitors out; not done going down — wait for stabilization.
Positive analyst note and price target increase today; 5% yield attractive; Kimberly tie-up with Canview expected to produce terrific results — buy.
Liquor sector (gins, vodkas, browns) all performing terribly; despite being best-in-class, sector headwinds too strong; don't try to call bottom — avoid entire group.
Outsourced services business but Cramer needs to research further; no immediate recommendation — comeback later with more analysis.
Beat and raised on exceptional execution; $210B backlog (4 years of revenue); Farnborough catalyst next week; down 10% from peak despite 22% earnings growth — rare buying window.
3-year backlog for data center power solutions; too constrained near-term; look at Quanta (QNTA) instead for more available capacity and better entry point.

Parabolic rally ($77 to $207) example of leverage-driven excess; when fundamentals divorce from stock price, professionals take profits; have to cut positions in half at peaks; current unwind likely to destroy even good fundamentals.

Terrific beat-and-raise: revenue +19%, EPS +45% YoY, operating ratio improved; intermodal loads at record, cadence strengthening; freight capacity shrinking, demand rising — first real cycle turn signal in decade; trading 38x earnings but story fantastic.

Triple-A balance sheet; oncology franchise best-in-class; neurology (Spravato, Caplyta, Icotide) unexpectedly strong; ophthalmology heating up; $150M heart miss immaterial on $100B revenue base; down $20 from highs.
For tech-heavy investor diversification; echoes 2000 dot-com reversal playbook (investors moved to Coke, Bristol Myers); defensive, stable dividend business.
Perfect quarter: women's division strength, DTC growing, all KPIs beat; temporarily crushed but rebounding; remains too cheap relative to earnings quality.
Semiconductor in margin death spiral; wait days for margin players purged; bottom not yet in — timing unclear.
Memory chip maker with strong demand but caught in margin death spiral; if borrowing money to buy, you still have chance to exit with shirt on; persist and naked by Monday.

Very good company but at nexus of margin craziness; hedge funds in trouble owning it; margin buyers using leverage; too many ill-positioned speculators; not done going down — another time to buy, not now.
Even large-cap semis like Nvidia caught in margin unwind; professionals selling huge chunks; no bottom yet visible; margin clerks not done with work.
Great company but perilous stock in hedge fund short attack; margin call cascade destroying valuation; fundamentals strong but stock too risky now.
Cost basis irrelevant; stock going down; too risky for IRA portfolio — sell and redeploy into more conservative holdings.
Electrical components for data center buildouts; fallen from 788 to 630; better entry than Wesco (WCC) — buy for data center infrastructure play.
CEO Chris Calio leading terrific execution; mix of defense and commercial attractive; recently in 170s, reports July 23; hold position and wait for pullback toward 170 before adding.
Memory chip company; if borrowing money to buy, you're part of margin plague; faster we purge these leveraged buyers, healthier market becomes.
Great company but perilous stock in margin unwinding; hedge funds shorting bad young managers; margin buyers forced to sell at bad prices — wait for clarity.
Good company but storage chip stocks caught in hedge fund leverage unwind; margin traders being blown out; avoid being part of panic selling.

Excellent margin improvement, great pricing, Optum recovery, Steve Hemsley returned as CEO restoring leadership; up 28% YTD but well below 2-year highs; gave up $40 opening gain — fantastic quarter, stock weakness opportunity.
At all-time high but not concern; great management; dominant position in oligopoly; not a semiconductor maker — own the stock.
Data center speculative play being margined out; wait for better prices; not far off but professionals still selling.
Quarter terrific but savaged by analysts obsessed with NIM/NII; CEO Scharf transforming into merchant bank winning in M&A, IPOs; trades 12x earnings — told Investing Club it's a steal.
